mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal, with lower rates directly supporting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.81% as of 2008-09-15 indicates aggressive easing already underway
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 2%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 6% signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity sell-offs due to reduced consumer spending power and corporate profit expectations. The mechanism activates when UNRATE crosses above 6% and predicts negative returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings forecasts.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of 2008-09-15 indicates threshold already breached
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following UNRATE > 6% threshold breach, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when RSAFS shows negative momentum and predicts negative SPX returns over 20 trading days as earnings guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 375473.0M as of 2008-09-15 in context of financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of periods following RSAFS monthly declines > 1%, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) jointly decline, signaling synchronized supply-side and labor contraction. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both series to show negative momentum simultaneously, predicting severe negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 50k month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show weakness in 2008 financial crisis context
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or the joint signal has Rank IC < 0.08 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 900k units signals collapsing residential investment and construction employment, triggering negative wealth effects and reduced consumer spending. This regime-conditional mechanism operates during housing downturns and predicts negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 895.0k as of 2008-08-15 indicates housing crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of periods when HOUST < 900k, or the signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate move in opposite directions, with CPIAUCSL stable while FEDFUNDS declines sharply, indicating real rate compression that supports equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both conditions and predicts positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL remains within 0.3% of prior month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.88 and FEDFUNDS at 1.81% show divergence
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when real rates compress via FEDFUNDS decline with stable CPI, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month, signaling labor market weakness that reduces aggregate income and consumer confidence. This always-on trend signal predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings and growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137318.0k as of 2008-09-15 in context of financial crisis employment losses
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of months following PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS changes and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth slows below 1% annualized rate, signaling economic stagnation that compresses equity risk premiums and reduces earnings growth expectations. This regime-conditional mechanism activates during low-growth regimes and predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11727.4B as of 2008-04-15 in early stages of Great Recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of quarters when GDPC1 growth < 1%, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.
